cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. So, the question is asking why Babbage failed to continue his research work in 1828. The options are A through D. First, I need to recall the information from the passage provided. The relevant part is in the third paragraph. Let me read that again. The third paragraph says: "Babbage continued his work in London for four years. Then his health broke down, and he had to take a long holiday abroad. When he returned to London in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. Many bills remained unpaid. His chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with many expensive tools. For one year no work was done." So, the key points here are: - He had health problems and took a long holiday abroad. But when he returned in 1828, the immediate issues were that he was at the end of his resources (money), unpaid bills, and co-workers left with tools. The result was that for one year no work was done. The question is asking for the main reason he failed to continue his research in 1828. The options are: A) Poor health B) Ran out of money C) Co-workers argued and left D) Spent money on bills The passage says that when he returned in 1828, he was "at the end of his resources," meaning money. The next sentences mention unpaid bills and co-workers leaving. However, the main reason for failure to continue research would be the primary cause leading to no work being done. While co-workers leaving is a problem, the text seems to present the lack of money as the initial issue.
